rationale:
Intuition: The dedicated /chat page (chat.html) was built as a simplified clone of the floating widget in layout.html, but was never updated when attachments/clipboard-paste support was added to layout.html. Comparing the two: layout.html's floating form has hx-encoding="multipart/form-data", a file input, an attach button, an image preview div, clipboard-paste JS, and after-request cleanup for those fields. chat.html has none of that. Additionally, neither interface persists chat history — navigating away or refreshing wipes the log — and there's no clear button.

What I'd fix: Three structural gaps in chat.html: (1) missing image attach + clipboard paste parity with the floating widget, (2) no localStorage-based history persistence so the log survives page refresh, (3) no clear button to intentionally wipe the history.

How: Edit middleware/templates/chat.html only.
- Add hx-encoding="multipart/form-data" to the form.
- Add a hidden file input (name="image", accept="image/*", class="benson-image-input") inside the form.
- Add a preview div (class="benson-image-preview") above the input-group.
- Add the attach button (📎, class="benson-attach-btn") to the input-group alongside the mic button.
- Update hx-on::after-request to also clear the image input and preview div, and call a JS function to save the updated chat log innerHTML to localStorage key "benson_chat_full_history".
- Add a "Clear" button to the card-header that wipes the chat-log-full div and removes the localStorage key.
- Add a {% block scripts %} section with: (a) on DOMContentLoaded, restore chat log from localStorage if present; (b) attach button → file picker click; (c) file picker change → showPreview(); (d) text input paste → intercept image items, stuff into file input, showPreview(); (e) htmx:afterRequest listener on the form to persist innerHTML to localStorage after each response arrives.
